summary: DHH describes his decision to learn VIM after finding the folding capabilities in his usual editor (UltraEdit) lacking. He praises VIM 6's powerful multi-level folding features while acknowledging the steep learning curve and obscure interface. He reframes the difficulty as a worthwhile horizon-expanding investment rather than a barrier.
key_points: VIM 6's folding capabilities far surpass what UltraEdit offered, with multi-level folds and flexible manipulation
VIM's power comes at the cost of obscurity — hundreds of hotkeys and a radically different interface from Windows conventions
Reframing a steep learning curve as 'expanding your horizon' makes the investment feel worthwhile rather than punishing
money_quotes: VIM is an improved version of the Unix VI editor and comes with all the obscurity and power you would expect.
The incredible power of VIM comes with a hefty price of obscurity.
Learning VIM doesn't seem like such hard work if you're thinking of it as an expansion of your horizon.
key_insight: Powerful tools with steep learning curves become approachable when you reframe the effort as expanding your capabilities rather than fighting an obstacle.
